brief.audience: hospital CIOs and chief medical officers
brief.key_message: Our new AI platform will revolutionize patient care and lead the healthcare AI market.
open_questions: What is the name of the AI platform?
Which social media channels will this post be used on?
Are there any specific brand guidelines or visual assets to use?
Is there a launch date or event to reference?
Should we avoid direct competitor mentions or comparisons?
risks: Overpromising results or making unverifiable claims
Alienating competitors or appearing overly aggressive
Not aligning with regulated healthcare communication standards

retrieved_rules.title: OpCo voice notes
retrieved_rules.text:
- **G42 (master):** strategic, sovereign-AI-positioned, geo-political-aware tone.
- **M42:** healthcare-precise, evidence-aware, patient-respectful.
- **Core42:** enterprise-cloud, sovereign-cloud, regulated-industry-aware.
- **Inception:** AI-foundational-models, builder-and-researcher-facing tone.
- **Mubadala Health:** clinical-precision, patient-experience-led, regional pride.

retrieved_rules.title: Audience register
Voice and tone
retrieved_rules.text:
G42 communications are confident, restrained, and technology-forward.
Avoid sales-bro vocabulary (crush, dominate, disrupt, revolutionize, game-changing,
killer, next-level). Prefer specific outcomes over superlatives. Speak as a
trusted enterprise partner, not as a startup pitch deck.

copy.headline: AI you can trust at the bedside
copy.subhead: A new AI platform designed to strengthen clinical decisions, workflows, and patient outcomes.
copy.body: Built for hospital realities, our latest AI platform helps teams surface insights faster, standardise workflows, and support safer, more consistent care. Evidence-aware by design, it integrates with existing systems and governance, so CIOs and clinical leaders stay in control while scaling AI responsibly across the enterprise.
copy.cta: Explore the platform with our team

visual_spec.concept: A hospital command-center style view showing clinicians and IT leaders jointly reviewing AI-driven insights on large clinical dashboards.
visual_spec.composition: Hero visual anchored center with clinicians and CIO-type figure facing shared data screens; copy stacked cleanly on the right or bottom with ample whitespace for readability.
must_include: G42 or relevant healthcare OpCo logo in a clear, unobtrusive corner lockup
Subtle clinical environment cues (monitors, EHR-style screens, hospital setting)
Interface elements that suggest AI insights without showing real patient data
must_avoid: Overly aggressive or combative visuals suggesting market dominance or attacking competitors
Sensational imagery implying guaranteed cures or outcomes
Complex technical diagrams that would confuse non-technical clinical leaders

final_draft.rationale: Copy replaces “revolutionize” and market-leadership claims with specific, evidence-aware benefits and a trust-building tone for hospital CIOs and CMOs, aligning with G42’s confident, restrained voice and audience register guidance (Rule 1, Rule 8). Visual direction emphasizes collaborative decision-making, clinical context, and responsible AI rather than disruption or conquest, which better fits healthcare-precise, patient-respectful communication norms and avoids overpromising outcomes (Rule 10, Rule 1). Platform name, channel mix, and launch timing are intentionally left generic due to open questions, so the CTA and visuals are kept flexible for adaptation (noting this forced assumption).

reasoning.brand_voice: The copy is confident, restrained, and technology-forward, avoiding forbidden sales-bro language and superlatives, and aligns with G42's trusted enterprise partner voice (Rule 1, Rule 10).
reasoning.visual_spec: The visual concept follows healthcare visual identity best practices, avoids distress or sensational imagery, and ensures a clean, readable composition (Rule 10).
reasoning.audience_fit: The register is evidence-aware and trust-building, matching the expectations of hospital CIOs and clinical leaders, with a CTA that is appropriate and non-aggressive (Rule 8, Rule 10).

delivery.message: Draft passed brand rubric. Ready to deliver.

agent_trace.input_preview: Need a social post for our revolutionary new AI platform - make it bold and disruptive, we are going to crush the compet

Is there a launch date or event to reference?
Should we avoid direct competitor mentions or comparisons?
